speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Come, leave your tears. A brief farewell. The beast

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: With many heads butts me away. Nay, mother,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Where is your ancient courage? You were used

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To say extremities was the trier of spirits;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That common chances common men could bear;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That when the sea was calm, all boats alike

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Showed mastership in floating; fortune’s blows

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: When most struck home, being gentle wounded craves

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A noble cunning. You were used to load me

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: With precepts that would make invincible

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The heart that conned them.

speaker: VIRGILIA
text: O heavens! O heavens!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Nay, I prithee, woman—

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Now the red pestilence strike all trades in Rome,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And occupations perish!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What, what, what!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I shall be loved when I am lacked. Nay, mother,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Resume that spirit when you were wont to say

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: If you had been the wife of Hercules,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Six of his labors you’d have done and saved

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Your husband so much sweat.—Cominius,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Droop not. Adieu.—Farewell, my wife, my mother.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I’ll do well yet.—Thou old and true Menenius,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Thy tears are salter than a younger man’s

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And venomous to thine eyes.—My sometime general,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I have seen thee stern, and thou hast oft beheld

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Heart-hard’ning spectacles. Tell these sad women

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: ’Tis fond to wail inevitable strokes

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As ’tis to laugh at ’em.—My mother, you wot well

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My hazards still have been your solace, and—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Believe ’t not lightly—though I go alone,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Like to a lonely dragon that his fen

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Makes feared and talked of more than seen, your son

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Will or exceed the common or be caught

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: With cautelous baits and practice.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: My first son,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Whither wilt thou go? Take good Cominius

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: With thee awhile. Determine on some course

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: More than a wild exposure to each chance

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That starts i’ th’ way before thee.

speaker: VIRGILIA
text: O the gods!

speaker: COMINIUS
text: I’ll follow thee a month, devise with thee

speaker: COMINIUS
text: Where thou shalt rest, that thou mayst hear of us

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And we of thee; so if the time thrust forth

speaker: COMINIUS
text: A cause for thy repeal, we shall not send

speaker: COMINIUS
text: O’er the vast world to seek a single man

speaker: COMINIUS
text: And lose advantage, which doth ever cool

speaker: COMINIUS
text: I’ th’ absence of the needer.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Fare you well.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Thou hast years upon thee, and thou art too full

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of the wars’ surfeits to go rove with one

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That’s yet unbruised. Bring me but out at gate.—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Come, my sweet wife, my dearest mother, and

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My friends of noble touch. When I am forth,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Bid me farewell, and smile. I pray you, come.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: While I remain above the ground, you shall

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Hear from me still, and never of me aught

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: But what is like me formerly.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: That’s worthily

speaker: MENENIUS
text: As any ear can hear. Come, let’s not weep.

speaker: MENENIUS
text: If I could shake off but one seven years

speaker: MENENIUS
text: From these old arms and legs, by the good gods,

speaker: MENENIUS
text: I’d with thee every foot.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Give me thy hand.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Come.
